title: Real Estate Deals in Jackson MS: A Complete Investor's Guide for 2024
excerpt: Jackson, Mississippi is one of the most affordable real estate markets in the country, offering investors strong cash flow potential and deep inventory of distressed properties. This guide covers the best neighborhoods, deal types, and strategies for finding profitable real estate deals in Jackson MS.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Jackson MS
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Jackson MS. Explore top neighborhoods, average home prices, wholesale & rental strategies, and tips for out-of-state investors.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Jackson MS, Jackson Mississippi real estate investing, wholesale real estate Jackson MS, Jackson MS rental properties, fix and flip Jackson Mississippi, off-market deals Jackson MS, Jackson MS real estate market

sections.content: Jackson, Mississippi doesn't make many national headlines, but savvy real estate investors have been quietly building wealth here for years. With some of the lowest entry prices in the country and solid rental demand, real estate deals in Jackson MS offer a rare combination of affordability and cash flow potential that's hard to find in larger metros. Whether you're just getting started or you're an experienced investor looking to diversify, this market deserves a serious look. If you're new to the game, check out our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide before diving in.
Jackson MS Real Estate Market Overview

Jackson is Mississippi's capital city and its most populous, with a metro population of around 580,000 people. The city has faced well-documented challenges — population decline, infrastructure issues, and economic headwinds — but those same challenges create opportunity for investors who know what they're doing. Low prices mean low barriers to entry, and rental demand stays strong because a large portion of residents can't qualify for mortgages or simply prefer to rent.

The Jackson real estate market is firmly a buyer's market. Days on market tend to run longer than national averages, which gives investors more negotiating leverage. Distressed inventory is plentiful, meaning wholesalers, flippers, and buy-and-hold investors all have plenty of raw material to work with. The key is knowing how to filter the good deals from the money pits.
Average Home Prices in Jackson MS

As of 2024, the median home price in Jackson sits around $120,000 to $140,000 — well below the national median of roughly $415,000. Distressed and off-market properties routinely sell for $30,000 to $80,000, making wholesale assignments and fix-and-flip projects financially feasible even for investors with limited capital. Rent rates for single-family homes typically range from $800 to $1,400 per month depending on condition and neighborhood, which means gross rent multipliers and cash-on-cash returns can be highly attractive when you buy right.

Always run your numbers before making an offer. Use our free [ARV Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator) to estimate after-repair value quickly, and cross-check your offer with the [Wholesale Deal Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to make sure there's enough margin for everyone in the deal.
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Jackson MS
Jackson is not a uniform market. Like any city, some zip codes are goldmines and others are traps. Here's a breakdown of the areas investors most commonly target:
Fondren

Fondren is Jackson's arts district and one of its most revitalized neighborhoods. Properties here attract young professionals, artists, and medical workers from nearby University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC). Home values are higher than much of Jackson — typically $150,000 to $250,000 — but so is tenant quality and stability. Fix-and-flip projects in Fondren can yield strong returns when bought at the right price.
Belhaven

Belhaven is one of Jackson's most historic and sought-after neighborhoods. Located close to Baptist Medical Center and Millsaps College, it draws a steady stream of healthcare workers and students. Properties here hold value better than most of Jackson, and renovated rentals can command premium rents between $1,100 and $1,600 per month.
North Jackson / Northside

North Jackson offers some of the market's most stable single-family rental inventory. The area has better school ratings than much of the city, which attracts family tenants who tend to stay longer. Cash flow is solid, and entry prices are still reasonable — typically $80,000 to $160,000 depending on condition.
South and West Jackson

These areas carry higher risk but also higher potential rewards for experienced investors. Prices can drop well below $50,000 for distressed properties, but vacancy rates are higher and property management is more challenging. Wholesale deals are abundant here, but due diligence needs to be thorough.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Jackson MS
Wholesale Deals

Wholesaling is extremely active in Jackson. The high volume of distressed, vacant, and probate properties makes it easy to find motivated sellers willing to accept below-market offers. A typical wholesale assignment in Jackson might involve a property with an ARV of $100,000, purchased under contract for $35,000 to $45,000, and assigned to a cash buyer for a $5,000 to $15,000 fee. If you're new to this strategy, our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide is a great place to start.
Fix and Flip

Jackson has a healthy flip market, particularly in transitional neighborhoods like Fondren and Belhaven. Renovation costs vary widely depending on condition, but a light cosmetic flip in a decent area can be completed for $20,000 to $40,000 and sold for a profit of $25,000 to $50,000. The key is accurate ARV estimation and tight contractor management — cost overruns will eat your margin fast in a lower-price market.
Buy-and-Hold Rentals

This is where Jackson really shines. A renovated 3-bedroom home in North Jackson purchased for $90,000 and renting for $1,100 per month produces a gross yield of nearly 15% — a number almost impossible to find in most major markets. Cash flow after mortgage, taxes, insurance, and management can range from $200 to $500 per month per door, depending on financing structure and neighborhood.
'In real estate, you make your money when you buy, not when you sell. Markets like Jackson reward disciplined buyers who know their numbers and aren't afraid of markets that others overlook.'
Local Market Trends to Watch

items: Medical sector demand: UMMC is one of the state's largest employers and continues to grow, driving consistent rental demand near Fondren and Belhaven.

Slow but steady revitalization: City and state programs are directing funding toward infrastructure repair and neighborhood redevelopment, which could lift values in targeted areas over the next 5 to 10 years.
Investor competition is increasing: Out-of-state investors have discovered Jackson's yields, meaning the easiest off-market deals are getting picked up faster than they were 3 to 5 years ago.

Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers: Jackson has a significant Section 8 tenant pool. Investors who learn how to work with the program can secure guaranteed rent from the government, which reduces vacancy risk.
Flood zone awareness: Parts of Jackson are in FEMA flood zones, which increases insurance costs and complicates financing. Always check flood maps before making an offer.

sections.content: Tips for Out-of-State Investors Buying in Jackson MS

Jackson is a popular target for remote investors, but buying from a distance requires extra preparation. The good news is that the market is deep enough and deal flow strong enough to make it worthwhile — if you approach it correctly. Learning [how to find off-market real estate deals](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/off-market-real-estate-deals) is especially valuable here, since the best Jackson properties rarely hit the MLS.

items: Build a local boots-on-the-ground team: You need a reliable property manager, a local real estate attorney, a general contractor, and ideally a local investor-friendly agent before you close your first deal.
Visit in person at least once: Drive the neighborhoods you're considering. Jackson's blocks can change dramatically within a few streets. A virtual tour won't tell you everything.
Budget conservatively for repairs: Labor costs in Mississippi are lower than national averages, but material costs have risen. Pad your rehab budget by 15% to 20% as a buffer.

Use data to find deals remotely: Platforms like [AIWholesail's Jackson MS market hub](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/jackson-ms) aggregate distressed property data, owner information, and deal signals that help out-of-state investors identify opportunities without flying in blind.

Understand the landlord-tenant laws: Mississippi is generally considered a landlord-friendly state, but Jackson's local ordinances and housing court processes have their own quirks. Know the eviction timeline before you buy.

Analyze every deal rigorously: Low prices can make bad deals feel acceptable. They're not. Learn [how to analyze real estate deals like a pro](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/how-to-analyze-real-estate-deals) to make sure every acquisition meets your minimum return thresholds.

sections.content: When evaluating rentals in Jackson, target a minimum 1% rent-to-price ratio as a starting filter — meaning a $100,000 property should rent for at least $1,000 per month. In many Jackson neighborhoods, you can beat that threshold significantly, which is a strong indicator of positive cash flow potential.
Is Jackson MS the Right Market for You?

Jackson isn't for every investor. The city's challenges are real, and this market rewards patience, local knowledge, and operational discipline. But for investors who do their homework, build the right team, and buy at the right price, Jackson offers cash flow yields and wholesale margins that are genuinely difficult to find elsewhere in 2024. The investors who succeed here aren't gambling — they're buying with a margin of safety and letting the numbers do the work.
